mission.title: Install and Configure Openfeature on Kubernetes
mission.description: OpenFeature is an open specification that provides a vendor-agnostic, community-driven API for feature flagging, allowing integration with various feature flag management tools. Installing OpenFeature enables you to manage feature flags effectively across your applications.

steps.title: Add the Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the OpenFeature Helm repository to your local Helm client to access the OpenFeature Operator chart.
```bash
helm repo add open-feature-operator https://open-feature.github.io/open-feature-operator
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install the OpenFeature Operator
steps.description:
Install the OpenFeature Operator using Helm in a new namespace called 'openfeature'.
```bash
helm install open-feature-operator open-feature-operator/open-feature-operator --namespace openfeature --create-namespace --version 0.8.9
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the OpenFeature Operator pods are running successfully.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace openfeature
```

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
Configure resource limits for the OpenFeature Operator to ensure it runs efficiently.
```bash
kubectl patch deployment open-feature-operator -n openfeature --type='json' -p='[{"op": "add", "path": "/spec/template/spec/containers/0/resources", "value": {"limits": {"cpu": "500m", "memory": "512Mi"}, "requests": {"cpu": "250m", "memory": "256Mi"}}}]'
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the OpenFeature Operator release from the cluster.
```bash
helm uninstall open-feature-operator --namespace openfeature
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and resources
uninstall.description:
Delete any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) associated with OpenFeature.
```bash
kubectl delete crd featureflags.openfeature.dev
kubectl delete crd featureflagproviders.openfeature.dev
```

uninstall.title: Remove the namespace
uninstall.description:
Delete the 'openfeature' namespace to clean up all resources.
```bash
kubectl delete namespace openfeature
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Export the current configuration and resources before upgrading.
```bash
kubectl get all --namespace openfeature -o yaml > openfeature-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Ensure your Helm repository is up to date with the latest charts.
```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade the OpenFeature Operator
upgrade.description:
Upgrade the OpenFeature Operator to the latest version.
```bash
helm upgrade open-feature-operator open-feature-operator/open-feature-operator --namespace openfeature --version 0.8.9
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check if the OpenFeature Operator pods are running the new version successfully.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace openfeature
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in Pending state
troubleshooting.description:
If the OpenFeature Operator pods are stuck in a Pending state, check for resource availability in your cluster.
```bash
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> --namespace openfeature
```

troubleshooting.title: Helm release failed
troubleshooting.description:
If the Helm installation fails, check the error message for details and ensure that the Helm repository is correctly added.
```bash
helm list --namespace openfeature
```

troubleshooting.title: Configuration issues
troubleshooting.description:
If the OpenFeature Operator is not functioning as expected, review the logs for errors.
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace openfeature
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of OpenFeature will result in the OpenFeature Operator running in the 'openfeature' namespace with all pods in the Running state. You can manage feature flags through the provided API once the installation is complete.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add open-feature-operator https://open-feature.github.io/open-feature-operator
helm repo update

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or higher and have Helm and kubectl installed.
